Here are the results for the PatternFly 6 migration guide official documentation:

## Official PatternFly 6 Migration Guide

The official PatternFly 6 upgrade guide is available at **https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/**

### Key Migration Resources

The upgrade guide outlines steps required to migrate your codebase to PatternFly 6 and includes notable changes (significant changes you should be aware of before starting), upgrade steps (step-by-step instructions), and help resources (easy-access links to get help from the PatternFly team).

### Notable Component Changes

Some significant changes include: Chip has been replaced with Label, Tile has been replaced with Card, and Text has been renamed to "Content".

### Codemods Available

PatternFly provides several codemods to assist with migration:

**class-name-updater** automatically identifies and renames classes to align with changes in PatternFly 6, and **tokens-update** updates global CSS variables in your React code, including React tokens.(no content)**css-vars-updater** updates CSS variables in non-React files, including .css, .scss, .md, or another file type that you specify.

### Upgrade Steps

The key steps are: Update your dependencies to use PatternFly 6 instead of PatternFly 5, run the codemods suite, remove all CSS overrides, review and update variable and class names, and update any pixel-based logic for your breakpoints.(no content)To run the codemods: `npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest <path to your source code> --v6`

### Design Token System

The new design token system in PatternFly 6 changes variable names across PatternFly. Any existing CSS overrides will be targeting outdated styles and will no longer work, so they must be updated or removed.

### Additional Resources

- Comprehensive PatternFly 6 release notes can be found on GitHub, in @patternfly/patternfly and @patternfly/react-core.
- While light and dark theming has previously been possible, PatternFly 6 makes it easier, and now both light and dark themes ship with PatternFly by default.

# React 19 Migration Status for PatternFly 6

## Current Status
The React 19 migration initiative is **in progress and on schedule** as of the latest update (April 28, 2025). All 22 sub-issues have been completed, with the effort targeted for the 2025.Q2 milestone (due July 2, 2025).

## Key Timeline & Goals
The initiative focuses on non-breaking changes to ensure PatternFly 6 remains compatible with React 17, 18, and 19. As stated in the roadmap: "The PatternFly React team will complete all non-breaking changes in order be compatible with React 19."

A second, separate initiative addresses breaking changes that will leverage features supported in React 18 & 19.

## Completed Work
All sub-issues (22/22) have been closed, including:
- JSX transform modernization
- PropTypes removal from the library
- DefaultProps deprecation handling
- Legacy Context API elimination
- React test utilities migration
- ReactDOM.render and findDOMNode removal
- TypeScript type cleanup
- useRef argument requirements

## Compatibility Approach
PatternFly follows a two-version support policy: "PF6 must support both React 17 & 18," limiting which modern React features can be utilized during this phase.

**Point of Contact:** @thatblindgeye (React team)

content.content:
# PatternFly 6 Upgrade Guide Summary

## Notable Breaking Changes

**Component Replacements:**
- "Chip component has been replaced with Label" 
- Tile replaced with Card
- Text component renamed to Content
- Expandable section: removed `isActive` prop
- Empty state: significant refactoring (may require manual updates)

**Chart Library Migration:**
Victory-based charts moved to a dedicated directory. Import paths changed from `'@patternfly/react-charts'` to `'@patternfly/react-charts/victory'`. Victory is now an optional peer dependency.

## Required Upgrade Steps

The upgrade process involves four main phases:

1. **Run codemods suite** - Execute PatternFly's automated codemods before manual changes using `npx @patternfly/pf-codemods@latest <path> --v6 --fix`

2. **Remove CSS overrides** - All existing custom CSS targeting PatternFly 5 styles must be updated or removed due to new design token system

3. **Update variable/class names** - Review custom CSS using three additional codemods: class-name-updater, tokens-update, and css-vars-updater

4. **Update breakpoint logic** - PatternFly 6 uses rem units instead of pixels (divide pixel values by 16)

## React Version Requirements

The documentation does not specify explicit React version requirements for PatternFly 6.

## Additional Support

The PatternFly team offers assistance via Slack and GitHub Discussions for upgrade-related questions.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about PatternFly 6 components in the `@patternfly/react-core` package:

## PatternFly 6 React Core Components

The `@patternfly/react-core` library provides a set of common React components for use with the PatternFly reference implementation. The latest version is 6.4.0.

### React Support
PatternFly supports the 2 most recent versions of React at a time. PatternFly 6 currently supports React 17 and 18, with support for React 19 coming soon.

### Available Packages
PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components that you can use to build interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. It is composed of several npm packages:
- `@patternfly/react-core`: Most React core components
- `@patternfly/react-charts`: PatternFly charting components
- `@patternfly/react-code-editor`: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor
- `@patternfly/react-icons`: PatternFly icons as React components
- `@patternfly/react-tokens`: PatternFly's CSS variables, transformed into JavaScript objects for use with React

### Key Components

**Modal Component:**
The `<ModalBody>`, `<ModalHeader>`, and `<ModalFooter>` components are not required, but are typically used to display the main content of the modal, modal title, and any button actions, respectively.

- To flag an open modal, use the `isOpen` property. To execute a callback when a modal is closed, use the `onClose` property.
- To adjust the size of a modal, use the `variant` property. Modal variants include "small", "medium", "large", and "default".
- To collect user input within a modal, you can add a form.

**Alert Component:**
PatternFly supports several alert variants for different scenarios. Each variant has an associated status icon, background, and alert title coded to communicate the severity of an alert. Use the `variant` property to apply styling options. If no variant is specified, then the variant will be set to "custom".

Alert variants include: custom, info, success, warning, and danger.

### Installation
Start using @patternfly/react-core in your project by running `npm i @patternfly/react-core`.(no content)Each PatternFly component already contains the CSS related to the component. In order to have consistent styles throughout the application, PatternFly Base CSS should be used in the project. This import statement should be placed before your main application component is imported:

```javascript
import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css';
```

For a complete list of all components and their documentation, visit the official [PatternFly website](https://www.patternfly.org/components/).

- To flag an open modal, use the `isOpen` property. To execute a callback when a modal is closed, use the `onClose` property.
- To adjust the size of a modal, use the `variant` property. Modal variants include "small", "medium", "large", and "default".
- To collect user input within a modal, you can add a form.

**Alert Component:**
PatternFly supports several alert variants for different scenarios. Each variant has an associated status icon, background, and alert title coded to communicate the severity of an alert. Use the `variant` property to apply styling options. If no variant is specified, then the variant will be set to "custom".

Alert variants include: custom, info, success, warning, and danger.

### Installation
Start using @patternfly/react-core in your project by running `npm i @patternfly/react-core`.(no content)Each PatternFly component already contains the CSS related to the component. In order to have consistent styles throughout the application, PatternFly Base CSS should be used in the project. This import statement should be placed before your main application component is imported:

```javascript
import '@patternfly/react-core/dist/styles/base.css';
```

For a complete list of all components and their documentation, visit the official [PatternFly website](https://www.patternfly.org/components/).

content.content:
# PatternFly Q2 & Q3 2025 Release Highlights

## Q2 2025 (PatternFly 6.3) - July 2025

**React 19 Support:**
PatternFly achieved comprehensive React 19 compatibility across all components and extensions. The update maintains backward compatibility with React 17 and 18, enabling teams to adopt React 19's features without disruption.

**Component Animations:**
The team implemented expansion animations (fade-in and slide-in transitions) across multiple components including Accordion, Expandable Section, Navigation, and Tree View. They also created an "enable-animations codemod" to streamline implementation in existing codebases.

**Key Features:**
- Interactive animations demo showcasing all supported motion effects
- Feedback animations for buttons, form validation, and tabs
- Icon-specific animations (favorite star, hamburger menu, settings cog)

## Q3 2025 (PatternFly 6.4) - October 2025

**High Contrast Mode (Beta):**
A beta version launching with higher-contrast colors and more prominent borders to support users with vision sensitivities. Full release planned for Q4 2025.

**Accessibility Enhancements:**
- Thicker, more prominent keyboard focus indicators on buttons
- Fixed data list action menu behavior
- Menu rendering improvements for screen readers
- Updated Jump Links and Skeleton component guidelines

**ChatBot Extension Improvements:**
Enhanced navigation, flexible layouts, advanced features including message dividers, editing capabilities, image previews, and transcript downloads.

**Resizable Table Columns:**
New feature in @patternfly/react-data-view allowing users to adjust column widths via click-and-drag interactions.

Here's the information I found about PatternFly 6 installation for `@patternfly/react-table` and `@patternfly/react-icons`:

## PatternFly 6 React Packages

PatternFly's React library contains a collection of components that you can use to build interfaces with consistent markup, styling, and behavior. It is composed of a number of React packages, which are published in the following npm packages: `@patternfly/react-core` for most React core components.

Additional packages include:
- `@patternfly/react-charts`: PatternFly charting components.
- `@patternfly/react-code-editor`: A PatternFly wrapper for the Monaco code editor.
- `@patternfly/react-icons`: PatternFly icons as React components.
- `@patternfly/react-tokens`: PatternFly's CSS variables, transformed into JavaScript objects for use with React.(no content)These packages are maintained together. To determine which versions of each package are compatible with the rest, view their release highlights.

## React Version Support

PatternFly supports the 2 most recent versions of React at a time. PatternFly 6 currently supports React 17 and 18, with support for React 19 coming soon.(no content)PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions, enabling you to benefit from new React features and performance enhancements. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

## Using @patternfly/react-icons

PatternFly Icons are available as React Components. You can import them like this: `import TimesIcon from '@patternfly/react-icons/dist/esm/icons/times-icon';` and use them as `const closeIcon = <TimesIcon />;`(no content)All icons from @patternfly/react-icons have the HTML class `pf-v6-svg` applied and CSS styles applied via @patternfly/react-styles.(no content)When using React, you can import 'pficon' icons from the react-icons package by including the following line: `import { [insert-icon-name] } from '@patternfly/react-icons/dist/esm/icons/[insert-hyphenated-icon-name]';`

## Q2 2025 Updates

For the Q2 2025 PatternFly release, progress was made in key initiatives, including PatternFly 6 migration enablement, component animations, React 19 support, and the continued evolution of PatternFly AI.(no content)As products have continued their PatternFly 6 migration journeys, they've received helpful feedback on the upgrade process, including common roadblocks. They expanded and reorganized the PatternFly 6 upgrade guide to more clearly and comprehensively support the upgrade process.

## Installation

Install a package manager before using the PatternFly libraries. You can install the packages via npm:

```bash
npm install @patternfly/react-core @patternfly/react-table @patternfly/react-icons
```

Comprehensive PatternFly 6 release notes can be found on GitHub, in @patternfly/patternfly and @patternfly/react-core.

## Overview
PatternFly is planning to enable React 19 features as a follow-up to their React 19 migration initiative (Issue #201).

## Planned Spikes

The initiative outlines three research areas:

1. **Feature Adoption**: "Define what new features PatternFly could adopt to improve the PatternFly library."

2. **Deprecation Research**: "Research what features we will need to deprecate by moving off of React 17."

3. **Code Mods**: "Create expected code mods issues and link as sub-issues."

The issue is labeled as a "Breaking change 💥," indicating that upgrading to React 19 will involve breaking changes for PatternFly users.

## Current Status
- **Type**: Initiative (long-term strategic goal)
- **Status**: Open
- **Progress**: 0 of 1 sub-issue completed
- **Created**: March 26, 2025

The initiative references the broader React 19 migration work and appears to be in the planning/research phase, with specific implementation details yet to be determined through the proposed spikes.

## @patternfly/react-core Versions 6.3 and 6.4 Information

The current version of @patternfly/react-core is 6.4.0, published approximately 2 months ago.

### Release Highlights for Version 6.4.0

The PatternFly 6.4 release includes @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0 along with several related packages. A new application-level context provider for enabling animations was introduced. You can now set the animation context at the root of your application to enable all opt-in animations by default.

### Key Updates in Recent Releases

PatternFly now includes support for React 19 across all components and extensions. This update is non-breaking, and PatternFly 6 will continue to support React 17 and 18 in line with their support policy.

### Recent Changes to be Aware Of

The column management and column management with draggable features have been rewritten to replace the use of the deprecated `<DragDrop>` component. You need to remove the deprecated `<DragDrop>` component by referring to the updated table demos.(no content)DOMpurify is no longer a dependency. You should uninstall/remove DOMpurify from your codebases.(no content)`<MessageBar>` is now a PatternFly `<TextArea>`, which leads to type changes for `<MessageBarProps>`. You need to update the Typescript types for `<MessageBarProps>` where relevant.

### Where to Find Comprehensive Changelogs

There is a resource that organizes the PatternFly 5 to PatternFly 6 change log in a searchable, filterable table. Note that this table does not contain a completely comprehensive list of changes. Comprehensive PatternFly 6 release notes can be found on GitHub, in @patternfly/patternfly and @patternfly/react-core.

For the most detailed changelog information for versions 6.3 and 6.4, I recommend checking the GitHub CHANGELOG.md file directly at: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/blob/main/packages/react-core/CHANGELOG.md

The search results didn't reveal specific breaking changes between minor versions 6.3 and 6.4, which suggests these are non-breaking minor releases as expected with their release policy. Minor feature releases are available quarterly to introduce non-breaking changes, like new features and enhancements.

# PatternFly 6 + React 19 Research (January 2026)

## Official Sources
- Official Upgrade Guide: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/upgrade/
- Release Highlights: https://www.patternfly.org/get-started/release-highlights/
- React 19 Migration Issue: https://github.com/patternfly/pf-roadmap/issues/201
- React 19 Enhancements Initiative: https://github.com/patternfly/pf-roadmap/issues/239
- GitHub Repository: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react

## Version Information
- **@patternfly/react-core**: 6.4.0 (latest stable as of January 2026)
- **React compatibility**: ^17 || ^18 || ^19 (all three versions supported)
- **react-dom compatibility**: ^17 || ^18 || ^19

### Related Packages (should be version-matched):
| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| @patternfly/react-core | Core UI components |
| @patternfly/react-table | Table components |
| @patternfly/react-icons | Icon components |
| @patternfly/react-charts | Charting components (Victory-based) |
| @patternfly/react-code-editor | Monaco code editor wrapper |
| @patternfly/react-tokens | CSS variables as JS objects |
